revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Heda Jinja]][[ja:部田神社]] {{Expand Japanese|部田神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Heda Shrine | native_name = 部田神社 | image = (5)Heda jinjya, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Heda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.970787|138.791766}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|ca|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|es|Ōkuninushi|fa|اوکونینوشی|fr|Ōkuninushi|hu|Ókuninusi|id|Ookuninushi|it|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|ko|오오쿠니누시|mni|ꯑꯣꯀꯨꯅꯤꯅꯨꯁꯤ|pl|Ōkuninushi|pt|Ookuninushi|ru|Окунинуси|simple|Ōkuninushi|sr|Okuninuši|sv|Ōkuninushi|tr|Ōkuninushi|uk|Окунінуші|vi|Ōkuninushi|zh|大國主|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Heda Shrine'''|部田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 78 ** P155: {{ill|Q117091796|ltq=Q117091796|qid=Q117091796|1=Ugusu Shrine}} ** P156: شیزوئوکا|fi|Numazu|fr|Numazu|ga|Numazu|gl|Numazu|hr|Numazu|id|Numazu, Shizuoka|io|Numazu, Shizuoka|it|Numazu|ja|沼津市|javoyage|沼津市|ko|누마즈시|mg|Numazu|mn|Нүмазү|mzn|نومازو، شیزوئوکا|nl|Numazu|no|Numazu|pl|Numazu|pt|Numazu|ro|Numazu|ru|Нумадзу|sh|Numazu|simple|Numazu|sr|Нумазу|sv|Numazu|szl|Numazu|tg|Нумазу|th|นูมาซุ|tl|Numazu|tr|Numazu|tt|Нумадзу|uk|Нумадзу (Сідзуока)|ur|نومازو، شیزوکا|vi|Numazu, Shizuoka|war|Numazu, Shizuoka|zh_classical|沼津市|zh_min_nan|Numazu-chhī|zh_yue|沼津|zh|沼津市|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html * {{ill|Q860928|ltq=Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} * {{ill|Naka district|lt=Naka district|ja|那賀郡 (静岡県)|qid=Q11642882|1=Naka district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.970787|138.791766}} == image (P18) == * (5)Heda jinjya, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * へだじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 410-3402https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 17753887 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8080105000541https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Commons category (P373) == * Heda Jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市戸田1585', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == image of entrance (P9721) == * Heda jinjya Torii, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg {{commons category|Category:Heda Jinja}} [[JA:部田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q92443788}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Heda Shrine |Image = [[File:(5)Heda jinjya, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg|280px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|58|15|N|138|47|30|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}{{ill|lt=Heda Village (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q5697064|qid=Q5697064|lt=Heda|1=Heda, Shizuoka}} 1585 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage DMS|34|58|15|N|138|47|30|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}, {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festival = October Sunday |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Heda Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|15|N|138|47|30|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Heda Shrine''' (Heda Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Heda Village (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q5697064|qid=Q5697064|lt=Heda|1=Heda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is the local tutelary shrine (ujigami) of the area and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}''' Formerly known as Buta-gami, Buta-no-za-gami, and Heta no Myōjin, it was an {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local ancestral deity). In ancient times it was an object of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|qid=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}} worship ({{ill|lt=Taishi faith|Q112695190|qid=Q112695190|1=Taishi cult}}), but as of the 21st century the principal enshrined deity is Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. It is the Shikinaisha “Buta Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and has a history of over 1,000 years. While {{ill|lt=Moroguchi Shrine|Q116694076|qid=Q116694076|1=Morokuchi Shrine (Q116694076)}} on {{ill|lt=Mihama Cape|Q11489198|qid=Q11489198|1=Cape Mihama}} is said to protect Heda from the sea, this shrine is regarded as the tutelary deity protecting it from the mountain side, and toward the end of the Edo period it was also called “Mishima Myōjin.”{{Cite book|Japanese|author=Editorial Committee of the History of Toda Village |title=History of Toda Village: General History |publisher=Numazu City |date=2016 |page=141 |isbn=}} In the Izu Province Shrine Rank Register it is recorded as Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, “Heta no Myōjin.”{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/izu/heda_title.htm |title=Heda Shrine |publisher= |accessdate=2020-03-23}} It was positioned as the general guardian shrine of the West Izu region and worshipped as a god of good fortune and as a protector of agriculture, fishing, and commerce. It is also said that the neighboring Myōhō Renge-ji temple served as the shrine’s bettō (shrine-temple administrator). Ten ridge plaques dating from 1596 (Keichō 1) to 1823 (Bunsei 6) survive, revealing that at least ten reconstructions and repairs were carried out. In particular, the 1817 (Bunka 14) plaque lists many stewards, including the village headman Katsuro family and newly risen shipping merchants, indicating that the reconstruction took place during a period of prosperity in Heda Village.{{Cite book|Japanese|author=Editorial Committee of the History of Toda Village |title=History of Toda Village: General History |publisher=Numazu City |date=2016 |page=142 |isbn=}} === Grounds === The shrine is located roughly in the center of the Heda valley. According to shrine records, the grounds cover 424 tsubo. Several large trees such as {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|qid=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}}, {{ill|lt=Podocarpus macrophyllus|Q165063|qid=Q165063|1=Podocarpus macrophyllus}}, and {{ill|lt=Japanese hackberry|Q706339|qid=Q706339|1=Celtis sinensis}} grow naturally there. Among them, several large {{ill|lt=camphor trees|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} with distinctive burls have been designated Natural Monuments by Numazu City. === Shrine buildings === * Main hall (Honden) – {{ill|lt=Hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}, keyaki-wood {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, housed within a red protective structure. * Worship hall (Haiden) – Copper roofing, {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}. * Auxiliary/enshrined shrines (within the precincts) ** Hie Shrine – enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kushimikenu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. ** Hachiman Shrine – enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. ** Yachimata Shrine – enshrines {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. * Daijingū – enshrines {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q?|qid=Q?|lt=Ōhirume no Muchi}}. * Tatsuta Shrine – enshrines {{ill|lt=Shinatobe|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinatobe no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|qid=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. * Chūkonsha – enshrines {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|qid=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}; some sources record it as Umayado Shrine. File:Heda jinjya Torii, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg File:Heda jinjya Honden, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg File:(3)Heda jinjya Kusu, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg File:(2)Heda jinjya, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg File:(4)Heda jinjya, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg File:(1)Heda jinjya, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg === Festivals === The annual festival was formerly held on the 9th and 10th days of the 9th lunar month, but with the calendar reform it became October 9–10, and in recent years has been changed to a Sunday in October.{{Cite book|Japanese|author=Editorial Committee of the History of Toda Village |title=History of Toda Village: Folk Customs |publisher=Numazu City |date=2014 |page=344 |isbn=}} Eighteen districts take turns handling festival duties; districts with fewer parishioners cooperate, completing one full rotation over a little more than ten years. The main duties of the host districts include making {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|qid=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} (sacred ropes) and providing entertainment on the festival day.{{Cite book|Japanese|author=Editorial Committee of the History of Toda Village |title=History of Toda Village: Folk Customs |publisher=Numazu City |date=2014 |page=345 |isbn=}} Compared with the spring Benten festival held by the fishing association, this has traditionally been a modest festival, without kagura or floats. Each year, the shimenawa are adjusted: three large ropes 3.8 meters long for the torii, worship hall, and sanctuary; one medium rope 2.5 meters long for the Chūkonsha; and four small ropes 1.7 meters long for small shrines, the stage, and the water pavilion. Entertainment is performed on a stage. In the 20th century, when youth groups were active, the precinct office served as the stage, and youths from each branch competed with performances or hired entertainers. In the past, stallholders came from {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu|1=Numazu}} to set up booths, and there was even a revolving stage. In the 21st century, outside stalls have been reduced, with local chambers of commerce and women’s associations providing booths, songs, and performances. === Cultural properties === [[File:(2)Heda jinjya Kusu, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg|thumb|Large camphor tree with burls. The one in front is regarded as the sacred tree.]] ; Large burl-bearing camphor trees of Heda Shrine : Two large camphor trees within the precincts were designated Natural Monuments by Numazu City on March 28, 2006 (Heisei 18).Based on an on-site sign installed in 2008 by the Numazu City Board of Education. The northern tree (①), regarded as the sacred tree, has a height of 34.5 m, a girth at breast height of 6.7 m, and a root circumference of 12.7 m. The southern tree (②) has a height of 34.2 m, a girth of 6.2 m, and a root circumference of 11.3 m. ; Ema (votive tablets) : Two ema (paintings) inside the shrine were designated cultural properties by {{ill|lt=Heda Village (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q5697064|qid=Q5697064|lt=Heda Village|1=Heda, Shizuoka}} on April 21, 1983 (Shōwa 58). They are “Flowers and Birds” dedicated in 1819 (Bunsei 2) and “Heda Fire Brigade I-gumi New Year Parade” painted in 1889 (Meiji 13). File:Heda jinjya Ema 1819, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg|Flowers and Birds (dedicated in 1819) File:Heda jinjya Ema 1989, Heda, Shizuoka.jpg|Heda Fire Brigade I-gumi New Year Parade (1889) === On-site information === * Address: 〒410-3402 1585 Heda, {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402125 |title=Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |publisher= |accessdate=2020-03-23}} * Access (bus): Get off at “Miya-no-mae,” about a 1-minute walk (46 m), or “JA Heda Branch,” about a 6-minute walk (456 m).{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://yaokami.jp/1220998/ |title=Yaoyorozu no Kami |publisher= |accessdate=2020-03-23}} It is located about 500 meters east of {{ill|lt=Heda Fishing Port|Q11496540|qid=Q11496540|lt=Heda Port|1=Port of Heda}}, slightly south of {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Route 18 (Shuzenji–Heda Line)|Q11663339|qid=Q11663339|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road Route 18}}, and enshrined facing toward the harbor. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{reflist|2}} === References === * Editorial Committee of the History of Toda Village, *History of Toda Village: General History*, Numazu City, 2016. * Editorial Committee of the History of Toda Village, *History of Toda Village: Folk Customs*, Numazu City, 2014. === Related items === {{Commonscat}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402125 Heda Shrine] Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Heda Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Heda Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]
